title: 365 Daily Bible Verses (KJV)
note: Verses sourced from KJV (public domain). Use https://bible-api.com/{reference}?translation=kjv to fetch live from the API.

verses.text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.
For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.
Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.
For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.
For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God.
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength: this is the first commandment.
Be strong and of a good courage, fear not, nor be afraid of them: for the LORD thy God, he it is that doth go with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.
Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up.
Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.
He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the LORD require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith.
Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
The LORD bless thee, and keep thee.
He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love.
Thou shalt have no other gods before me.
But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.
And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.

If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.
Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
A man that hath friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.
Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God: I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee; yea, I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.
Rejoice evermore.
Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations.
Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.
There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.

When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee: when thou walkest through the fire, thou shalt not be burned; neither shall the flame kindle upon thee.
All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness.
He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men.
The LORD shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
O taste and see that the LORD is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.

But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.
When pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly is wisdom.
Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.
I will praise thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works; and that my soul knoweth right well.
Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD.
Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
I have set the LORD always before me: because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.
Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.

There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear it.
For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, saith the LORD.
Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears: behold, I will heal thee.
For with God nothing shall be impossible.
A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.
And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.
And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven.

For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come, nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
This is the day which the LORD hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
But as for you, ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good, to bring to pass, as it is this day, to save much people alive.
Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me.
In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you: and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you an heart of flesh.
And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.
Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.
Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
Salvation is of the LORD.
Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.
And the LORD shall guide thee continually, and satisfy thy soul in drought, and make fat thy bones: and thou shalt be like a watered garden, and like a spring of water, whose waters fail not.
Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.
Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad.
But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.
I will praise thee, O LORD, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear.
Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith.
That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.
For the LORD God is a sun and shield: the LORD will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.

I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.
A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver.
Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.
The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name.
Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.
Whither thou goest, I will go; and where thou lodgest, I will lodge: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God.
For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost.
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?
Remember ye not the former things, neither consider the things of old.
And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; and his greatness is unsearchable.
I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
Then shall we know, if we follow on to know the LORD: his going forth is prepared as the morning; and he shall come unto us as the rain, as the latter and former rain unto the earth.
Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us.
The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him.
After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.
The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
But be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.
But let judgment run down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream.
I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself.
I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.
I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.
To appoint unto them that mourn in Zion, to give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness.
That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth.
I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me.
As every man hath received the gift, even so minister the same one to another, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God.
Who is a God like unto thee, that pardoneth iniquity, and passeth by the transgression of the remnant of his heritage? he retaineth not his anger for ever, because he delighteth in mercy.
For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.
Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man.
Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.
Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.

Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us.
There are many devices in a man's heart; nevertheless the counsel of the LORD, that shall stand.
And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.
Let every thing that hath breath praise the LORD. Praise ye the LORD.

For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.
Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.
And he believed in the LORD; and he counted it to him for righteousness.
Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.
Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer.
I waited patiently for the LORD; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
For the joy of the LORD is your strength.
There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.
A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.

The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised.
For thou art my hope, O Lord GOD: thou art my trust from my youth.
I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the Almighty.
But to this man will I look, even to him that is poor and of a contrite spirit, and trembleth at my word.
But as it is written, Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him.
For as he thinketh in his heart, so is he.
Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it.
Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
He must increase, but I must decrease.
If it be so, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace, and he will deliver us out of thine hand, O king.
And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.
When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.
For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise: be thankful unto him, and bless his name.
For our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory.
And thine ears shall hear a word behind thee, saying, This is the way, walk ye in it, when ye turn to the right hand, and when ye turn to the left.
Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed.
Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth.
For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.
But now thus saith the LORD that created thee, O Jacob, and he that formed thee, O Israel, Fear not: for I have redeemed thee, I have called thee by thy name; thou art mine.
But Jesus beheld them, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.
A man's heart deviseth his way: but the LORD directeth his steps.
Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.
O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
The LORD thy God in the midst of thee is mighty; he will save, he will rejoice over thee with joy; he will rest in his love, he will joy over thee with singing.
Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.
For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life.
But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of darkness into his marvellous light.
The steps of a good man are ordered by the LORD: and he delighteth in his way.
Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.
Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of my hands; thy walls are continually before me.
Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.
Even so faith, if it hath not works, is dead, being alone.
O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee in a dry and thirsty land, where no water is.
And God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work.
Blessed is the man that trusteth in the LORD, and whose hope the LORD is.
For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.
Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.
It is more blessed to give than to receive.
LORD, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.
In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, according to the riches of his grace.
For the mountains shall depart, and the hills be removed; but my kindness shall not depart from thee, neither shall the covenant of my peace be removed, saith the LORD that hath mercy on thee.
I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the LORD pondereth the hearts.
Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
Let every soul be subject unto the higher powers. For there is no power but of God: the powers that be are ordained of God.
Shew me thy ways, O LORD; teach me thy paths.
Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
My little children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth.
For Ezra had prepared his heart to seek the law of the LORD, and to do it, and to teach in Israel statutes and judgments.
But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.
Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the LORD, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.
He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.
A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favour rather than silver and gold.
But he said, Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.
The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
The LORD is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.
The name of the LORD is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.
Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.
Praise ye the LORD. Blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.
I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith.
So shall my word be that goeth forth out of my mouth: it shall not return unto me void, but it shall accomplish that which I please, and it shall prosper in the thing whereto I sent it.

He saith unto him the third time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him.
The pride of thine heart hath deceived thee, thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, whose habitation is high; that saith in his heart, Who shall bring me down to the ground?
And they said, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved, and thy house.
He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.
And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.
Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.
Teach me to do thy will; for thou art my God: thy spirit is good; lead me into the land of uprightness.
God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe.
For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.
Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the LORD is risen upon thee.
Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions.
Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
The liberal soul shall be made fat: and he that watereth shall be watered also himself.
Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and stedfast, and which entereth into that within the veil.
For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain.

For thus saith the high and lofty One that inhabiteth eternity, whose name is Holy; I dwell in the high and holy place, with him also that is of a contrite and humble spirit, to revive the spirit of the humble, and to revive the heart of the contrite ones.
For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
The soul of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.
Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
They that trust in the LORD shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever.
And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of their testimony; and they loved not their lives unto the death.
The LORD God is my strength, and he will make my feet like hinds' feet, and he will make me to walk upon mine high places.
And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.
Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom.
The LORD hear thee in the day of trouble; the name of the God of Jacob defend thee.
Confess your faults one to another, and pray one for another, that ye may be healed. The effectual fervent prayer of a righteous man availeth much.
Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust, and not be afraid: for the LORD JEHOVAH is my strength and my song; he also is become my salvation.
And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.
Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.
He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven.
He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God.
Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.
Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal.

Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse, that there may be meat in mine house, and prove me now herewith, saith the LORD of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it.
Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.
The LORD will give strength unto his people; the LORD will bless his people with peace.
For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.
But now, O LORD, thou art our father; we are the clay, and thou our potter; and we all are the work of thy hand.
By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.

For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
